description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, layered texture and a predominantly gray coloration interspersed with white and darker streaks. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible striations and natural veining that suggest a metamorphic origin, such as gneiss or schist. A small blue label with black writing is affixed to one of the flatter surfaces, indicating it may be cataloged or studied in an urban or academic context. The stone's edges are jagged and angular, with some faces appearing more weathered and granular than others. Its mass and density are apparent from the compact, solid appearance, and it would be difficult to move without effort. This stone would likely serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park, sidewalk, or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, gray stone with layered striations, rough texture, and a blue label affixed to one surface. Its irregular shape and dense appearance make it a prominent obstacle or decorative feature.
description_view_0: The stone is seen from a broad, flat side with pronounced horizontal striations and the blue label clearly visible near the top.
description_view_1: This view shows a more angular, pointed end of the stone, with the blue label on a sloped surface and the rough, granular texture prominent.
description_view_2: The stone appears elongated and low, with the layered pattern running lengthwise and the blue label just visible on the upper surface.
description_view_3: A corner view highlights the stone's jagged edge and the transition between rough and smoother surfaces, with the blue label partially visible.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or roadside verge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or natural obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Distinct horizontal veining and layering with irregular mottling and streaks, giving a natural, banded appearance.
attribute_weathering: Rough and granular with some smoother faces, indicating moderate weathering but no significant erosion or lichen growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; the blue label adds a man-made touch.
